For this production system, permanent pasture producing Beef cattle, calf (male, weaned) in France. Additional description: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing., what is the appropriate allocation method? Possible answers are: economic, mass, energy, biophysical, none, none required, system expansion.
